mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing three months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.49% in Nov 2007 vintage, down from 5.25% mid-2007
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20d forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive returns fail to materialize in more than 55% of easing episodes since 2005.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing three-month window, this signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that precedes broader earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% Nov 2007, up from 4.4% cycle low earlier in 2007
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where the UNRATE trigger fires, or the trigger fires fewer than 10 times in the 2005-2024 sample making the test underpowered.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in housing starts and retail sales indicates simultaneous contraction in both durable and non-durable consumer demand channels, a combination more predictive of recessionary equity drawdowns than either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year decline > 15% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value 1229.0 Oct 2007 down sharply from 2005-2006 peaks above 2000
regime_hint: housing downturn coinciding with consumer demand weakness
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns are non-negative on average during joint-trigger periods, or the interaction term's marginal predictive contribution over either single-series model is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, decelerating industrial production growth signals an approaching output peak and margin compression for cyclical firms, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is muted or reversed during early-cycle recoveries when INDPRO deceleration reflects normalization rather than contraction.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth rate falls below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage growth slowing into late 2007
failure_condition: The negative return relationship fails to hold (average 60d forward return >= 0) when the trigger fires during independently identified late-cycle periods, or the sign of the effect flips depending on early- vs late-cycle labeling with no consistent late-cycle negative skew.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects continued labor income generation supporting near-term consumption and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to payroll print momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value 138467.0 thousand Nov 2007 still expanding modestly
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and 5d forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following positive payroll prints are not significantly higher than following negative prints (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation accelerates above a threshold while the Fed simultaneously cuts the funds rate, this combination signals a stagflationary policy bind in which the central bank cannot support growth without risking inflation credibility, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS falls over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value 210.565 Nov 2007 with rising YoY inflation amid FEDFUNDS cuts from 5.25% to 4.49%
regime_hint: stagflationary monetary easing amid rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are non-negative on average during joint-trigger periods, or the CPI-FEDFUNDS interaction shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone (delta Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-quarter decline in housing starts during a late-cycle expansion is a leading indicator of broader demand contraction that transmits to corporate earnings with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the relationship is weak or absent during mid-cycle expansions where housing softness reflects supply-side rebalancing rather than demand collapse.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST declining from over 2000k units in 2005 to 1229.0 by Oct 2007
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns during periods of sustained HOUST decline in identified late-cycle windows are not significantly more negative than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the effect reverses sign in more than one of the historical late-cycle housing downturns in-sample.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth decelerates while payroll employment growth remains resilient indicates labor hoarding ahead of an anticipated output slowdown, a combination that precedes negative equity surprises as firms eventually cut headcount, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND PAYEMS 3-month change remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage growth weakening into late 2007 while PAYEMS still positive
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns during joint-trigger episodes are non-negative on average, or the two-series interaction fails to outperform a single-series INDPRO-only model by more than 0.02 Rank IC in walk-forward tests.
